title: anchusa undulata subsp. sartorii
intro: Anchusa undulata subsp. sartorii is a subspecies of the Anchusa undulata plant, commonly known as alkanet, which is a flowering plant in the borage family. The roots and flowers of the plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of using Anchusa undulata subsp. sartorii may include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of colds and respiratory infections, as well as potentially aiding in wound healing and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
